purpose: Report Toulmin critical-question COVERAGE for an argument-shaped nonfiction passage: for each major claim a pluggable LLM judge identifies, whether the warrant (inferential link), the backing for that warrant, and a rebuttal (counterargument / exception) are present, partial, or absent (Favero et al. 2024, "Critical Questions of Thought", arXiv:2412.15177). Descriptive coverage for a human reviewer — what questions the text answers vs leaves open. The warrant sibling of fallacy_scan under the same argument_pattern_scan surface; emits no soundness / quality / "unsound" label, score, or aggregate. An absent warrant/backing/rebuttal is a coverage GAP to examine, not a flaw.
use_when: You want per-claim warrant / backing / rebuttal coverage to triage for human review
Target is an argument-shaped nonfiction passage (op-ed / policy / testimony)
You want to see which Toulmin critical questions the text leaves open
do_not_use_when: You need a soundness / quality / "weak argument" / "unsound" verdict (the surface refuses ALL of these — coverage is descriptive; an absent warrant is a gap to examine, not a flaw, since many strong arguments leave a warrant implicit)
You want a coverage SCORE as a quality metric (coverage_summary is a descriptive per-question count, never a score)
You don't have an LLM judge or a pre-computed label manifest

compute.cost_note: $0.01–0.10 per doc via Anthropic / OpenAI / Gemini (one labeling call); free with --judge manifest or --judge mock (a deterministic test stub); key-free with --judge agent_host (delegates to the host runtime's model — Claude Code / Codex / Gemini Antigravity; see spec 35)

examples.description: Warrant-coverage probe with a pre-computed judge manifest
